class FormElement extends HTMLElement builder/form/FormElement.class.php
Package: Builder · Version: PHPBoost 6.1 - last update: 2015 01 19 · Authors: Benoit SAUTEL <ben.popeye, Kevin MASSY <reidlos
class HTMLForm implements HTMLElement builder/form/HTMLForm.class.php

This class enables you to handle all the operations regarding forms. Indeed, you build a form using object components (fieldsets, fields, buttons) and it's able to display, to retrieve the posted values and also validate the entered data from constraints you define. The validation is done in PHP when the form is received, but also in live thanks to Javascript (each field is validated when it looses the focus and the whole form is validated when the user submits it).

Package: Builder · Since: PHPBoost 3.0 - 2009 04 28 · Version: PHPBoost 6.1 - last update: 2024 01 31 · Authors: Regis VIARRE <crowkait, Julien BRISWALTER <j1.seth, Arnaud GENET <elenwii, Sebastien LARTIGUE <babsolune
Constants
HTTP_METHOD_POST 'post'
HTTP_METHOD_GET 'get'
► Methods (24)
public __construct($html_id, $target = '', $enable_captcha_protection = true)
This class enables you to handle all the operations regarding forms. Indeed, you build a form using object components (fieldsets, fields, buttons) and it's able to display, to retrieve the posted values and also validate the entered data from constraints you define. The validation is done in PHP when the form is received, but also in live thanks to Javascript (each field is validated when it looses the focus and the whole form is validated when the user submits it).

public move_captcha_protection_in_last_position()
public add_fieldset(FormFieldset $fieldset)
This class enables you to handle all the operations regarding forms. Indeed, you build a form using object components (fieldsets, fields, buttons) and it's able to display, to retrieve the posted values and also validate the entered data from constraints you define. The validation is done in PHP when the form is received, but also in live thanks to Javascript (each field is validated when it looses the focus and the whole form is validated when the user submits it).
public add_constraint(FormConstraint $constraint)
This class enables you to handle all the operations regarding forms. Indeed, you build a form using object components (fieldsets, fields, buttons) and it's able to display, to retrieve the posted values and also validate the entered data from constraints you define. The validation is done in PHP when the form is received, but also in live thanks to Javascript (each field is validated when it looses the focus and the whole form is validated when the user submits it).

class FormFieldSubTitle extends AbstractFormField builder/form/menu/FormFieldSubTitle.class.php

This class is a fork of FormFieldFree and manage subtitle for form with menu. It provides you additionnal field options : <ul> <li>template : A template object to personnalize the field</li> <li>content : The field html content if you don't use a personnal template</li> </ul>

class WizardHTMLForm extends HTMLForm builder/form/menu/WizardHTMLForm.class.php

This class is a child of HTMLForms for a wizard menu and enables you to handle all the operations regarding forms. Indeed, you build a form using object components (fieldsets, fields, buttons) and it's able to display, to retrieve the posted values and also validate the entered data from constraints you define. The validation is done in PHP when the form is received, but also in live thanks to Javascript (each field is validated when it looses the focus and the whole form is validated when the user submits it).

Package: Builder · Since: PHPBoost 5.2 - 2019 07 31 · Version: PHPBoost 6.1 - last update: 2019 11 03 · Authors: Sebastien LARTIGUE <babsolune, Julien BRISWALTER <j1.seth

class CategoriesManager content/category/CategoriesManager.class.php

This class allows you to easily integrate a complete system of categories in its script. It needs to have its first instantiation of a parameter child object CategoriesCache and as the second parameter to have a CategoriesItemsParameters object.

class SearchCategoryChildrensOptions content/category/SearchCategoryChildrensOptions.class.php

This class allows you to manage options of children used in CategoriesManager::get_children() and CategoriesManager::get_select_categories_form_field(). You will be able to manage one or more permission bits, exclude certain categories disable recursive search of one, several or all categories

class AbstractParser implements FormattingParser content/formatting/parser/AbstractParser.class.php

This class is the basis of all the formatting processings that exist in PHPBoost.

class BbcodeHighlighter extends AbstractParser content/formatting/parser/BBCodeHighlighter.class.php

This class is a highlighter for the PHPBoost Bbcode language. It supplies the highlighted code written in XHTML.

class ContentSecondParser extends AbstractParser content/formatting/parser/ContentSecondParser.class.php

This class ensures the real time processing of the content. The major part of the processing is saved in the database to minimize as much as possible the treatment when the content is displayed. However, some tags cannot be cached, because we cannot have return to the original code. It's for instance the case of the code tag which replaces the code by a lot of html code which formats the code. This kind of tag is treated in real time by this class. The content you put in that parser must come from a ContentFormattingParser class (BbcodeParser or TinyMCEParser) (it can have been saved in a database between the first parsing and the real time parsing).

Package: Content · Since: PHPBoost 2.0 - 2008 08 10 · Version: PHPBoost 6.1 - last update: 2022 06 01 · Authors: Benoit SAUTEL <ben.popeye, Julien BRISWALTER <j1.seth, Arnaud GENET <elenwii, mipel <mipel, janus57 <janus57, Sebastien LARTIGUE <babsolune, xela <xela

MAX_CODE_LENGTH 40000

class DefaultCategoryLobbyProvider extends DefaultModuleLobbyProvider content/lobby/DefaultCategoryLobbyProvider.class.php

Generic lobby category sub-view provider for any ItemsService-based module. Usage in ExtensionPointProvider::lobby(): return [ new DefaultItemsLobbyProvider('articles'), new DefaultCategoryLobbyProvider('articles'), ]; The module_id for the category entry will be '{module_id}_category'.

class Environment core/environment/Environment.class.php

This class manages all the environment that PHPBoost need to run. <p>It's able to initialize the environment that contains services (database, users management...) as well as the graphical environment.</p>

Package: Core · Since: PHPBoost 3.0 - 2009 09 28 · Version: PHPBoost 6.1 - last update: 2025 11 24 · Authors: Benoit SAUTEL <ben.popeye, Loic ROUCHON <horn, Julien BRISWALTER <j1.seth, Arnaud GENET <elenwii, janus57 <janus57, xela <xela, Sebastien LARTIGUE <babsolune

class CacheData io/data/cache/CacheData.class.php

This interface represents data which are stored automatically by the cache manager. The storage mode is very powerful, it uses a two-level cache and the database. <p>The cache manager is able to manager very well configuration values. They are stored in a map associating a value to a property</p>

Package: IO · Since: PHPBoost 3.0 - 2009 09 16 · Version: PHPBoost 6.1 - last update: 2014 12 22 · Authors: Benoit SAUTEL <ben.popeye

This interface represents data which are stored automatically by the cache manager. The storage mode is very powerful, it uses a two-level cache and the database. <p>The cache manager is able to manager very well configuration values. They are stored in a map associating a value to a property</p>
class CacheDataNotFoundException extends Exception io/data/cache/CacheDataNotFoundException.class.php

This exception is raised when you are asking a cache entry that doesn't exist.

Package: IO · Since: PHPBoost 3.0 - 2010 01 22 · Version: PHPBoost 6.1 - last update: 2014 12 22 · Authors: Benoit SAUTEL <ben.popeye
► Methods (1)
public __construct($config_name)
class CacheManager io/data/cache/CacheManager.class.php

This class manages cache. It makes a two-level lazy loading: <ul> <li>A top-level cache which avoids loading a data if it has already been done since the beginning of the current page generation. This cache has a short life span: it's flushed as of the PHP interpreter reaches the end of the page generation.</li> <li>A filesystem or shared RAM (via APC) cache to avoid querying the database every time to obtain the same value. This cache is less powerful than the previous but has an infinite life span. Indeed, it's valid until the value changes and the manager is asked to store it</li> </ul>

Package: IO · Since: PHPBoost 3.0 - 2009 09 16 · Version: PHPBoost 6.1 - last update: 2020 02 10 · Authors: Benoit SAUTEL <ben.popeye, Julien BRISWALTER <j1.seth
► Methods (17)

This cache has a short life span: it's flushed as of the PHP interpreter reaches the end of the page generation.</li> <li>A filesystem or shared RAM (via APC) cache to avoid querying the database every time to obtain the same value. This cache is less powerful than the previous but has an infinite life span. Indeed, it's valid until the value changes and the manager is asked to store it</li> </ul>

This cache is less powerful than the previous but has an infinite life span. Indeed, it's valid until the value changes and the manager is asked to store it</li> </ul>
static load($classname, $module_name, $entry_name = '')
This class manages cache. It makes a two-level lazy loading: <ul> <li>A top-level cache which avoids loading a data if it has already been done since the beginning of the current page generation. This cache has a short life span: it's flushed as of the PHP interpreter reaches the end of the page generation.</li> <li>A filesystem or shared RAM (via APC) cache to avoid querying the database every time to obtain the same value. This cache is less powerful than the previous but has an infinite life span. Indeed, it's valid until the value changes and the manager is asked to store it</li> </ul>
static try_load($classname, $module_name, $entry_name)
This class manages cache. It makes a two-level lazy loading: <ul> <li>A top-level cache which avoids loading a data if it has already been done since the beginning of the current page generation. This cache has a short life span: it's flushed as of the PHP interpreter reaches the end of the page generation.</li> <li>A filesystem or shared RAM (via APC) cache to avoid querying the database every time to obtain the same value. This cache is less powerful than the previous but has an infinite life span. Indeed, it's valid until the value changes and the manager is asked to store it</li> </ul>
static invalidate($module_name, $entry_name = '')

class ConfigData extends CacheData io/data/config/ConfigData.class.php

This interface represents configuration data which are stored automatically by the config manager. The storage mode is very powerful, it uses a two-level cache and the database. <p>They are stored in a map associating a value to a property</p>

Package: IO · Since: PHPBoost 3.0 - 2009 09 16 · Version: PHPBoost 6.1 - last update: 2024 08 22 · Authors: Benoit SAUTEL <ben.popeye, Julien BRISWALTER <j1.seth

public delete_property($name)
This interface represents configuration data which are stored automatically by the config manager. The storage mode is very powerful, it uses a two-level cache and the database. <p>They are stored in a map associating a value to a property</p>
class ConfigManager io/data/config/ConfigManager.class.php

This class manages config loading and saving. It makes a two-level lazy loading: <ul> <li>A top-level cache which avoids loading a data if it has already been done since the beginning of the current page generation. This cache has a short life span: it's flushed as of the PHP interpreter reaches the end of the page generation.</li> <li>A filesystem or shared RAM cache to avoid querying the database many times to obtain the same value. This cache is less powerful than the previous one but it has an infinite life span. Indeed, it's valid until the value changes and the manager is asked to store it</li> </ul>

Package: IO · Since: PHPBoost 3.0 - 2009 09 16 · Version: PHPBoost 6.1 - last update: 2021 11 04 · Authors: Benoit SAUTEL <ben.popeye, Julien BRISWALTER <j1.seth
► Methods (6)

This cache is less powerful than the previous one but it has an infinite life span. Indeed, it's valid until the value changes and the manager is asked to store it</li> </ul>

class APCDataStore implements DataStore io/data/store/APCDataStore.class.php

This data store is not already available, the APC PHP extension must be enabled for you yo use it. When it's available, it provides a memory area that is persistent (its life span is no the page execution) and shared by all simultaneous page executions. This is very efficient and has an infinite life span (in fact it's the Web server's one).

Package: IO · Since: PHPBoost 3.0 - 2009 09 16 · Version: PHPBoost 6.1 - last update: 2016 10 28 · Authors: Benoit SAUTEL <ben.popeye, Loic ROUCHON <horn, Julien BRISWALTER <j1.seth, Arnaud GENET <elenwii

class DataStore io/data/store/DataStore.class.php

This interface represents a data store. Its different implementations store data in different physical areas, you have to use it according to the data's life span and the efficiency you need. A container can store several pieces of data, each of one has a string identifier you choose. That identifier must be a string with only letters and digits.

Package: IO · Since: PHPBoost 3.0 - 2009 12 09 · Version: PHPBoost 6.1 - last update: 2014 12 22 · Authors: Benoit SAUTEL <ben.popeye

class FileSystemDataStore implements DataStore io/data/store/FileSystemDataStore.class.php

This data store is not very efficient but is the only one which has an infinite life span when APC is not available. It stores data in the /cache folder.

class RAMDataStore implements DataStore io/data/store/RAMDataStore.class.php

This is a very efficient data store, but its principal weakness is that it's life span is very short, in fact it's the page's execution. It's to use when you know that the data you want to store will be accessed several times during the page execution.

Package: IO · Since: PHPBoost 3.0 - 2011 01 11 · Version: PHPBoost 6.1 - last update: 2014 12 22 · Authors: Benoit SAUTEL <ben.popeye, Loic ROUCHON <horn

class FileTemplate extends AbstractTemplate io/template/FileTemplate.class.php

This class allows you to handle a template file. Your template files should have the .tpl extension. To be more efficient, this class uses a cache and parses each file only once. <h4>File identifiers</h4> The web site can have several themes whose files aren't in the same folders. When you load a file, you just have to load the generic file and the right template file will be loaded dynamically. <h5>Kernel template file</h5> When you want to load a kernel template file, the path you must indicate is only the name of the file, for example header.tpl loads /template/your_theme/header.tpl and if it doesn't exist, it will load /template/default/header.tpl. <h5>Module template file</h5> When you want to load a module template file, you must indicate the name of you module and then the name of the file like this: module/file.tpl which will load the /module/templates/file.tpl. If the user themes redefines the file.tpl file for the module module, the file templates/your_theme/modules/module/file.tpl will be loaded. <h5>Menu template file</h5> To load a file of a menu, use this kind of path: menus/my_menu/file.tpl which will load the /menus/my_menu/templates/file.tpl file. <h5>Framework template file</h5> To load a framework file, use a path like this: framework/package/file.tpl which will load /templates/your_theme/framework/package/file.tpl if the theme overrides it, otherwise /templates/default/framework/package/file.tpl will be used.

Package: IO · Since: PHPBoost 3.0 - 2009 06 18 · Version: PHPBoost 6.1 - last update: 2016 10 24 · Authors: Loic ROUCHON <horn, Regis VIARRE <crowkait, Arnaud GENET <elenwii
► Methods (2)

This class allows you to handle a template file. Your template files should have the .tpl extension. To be more efficient, this class uses a cache and parses each file only once. <h4>File identifiers</h4> The web site can have several themes whose files aren't in the same folders. When you load a file, you just have to load the generic file and the right template file will be loaded dynamically. <h5>Kernel template file</h5> When you want to load a kernel template file, the path you must indicate is only the name of the file, for example header.tpl loads /template/your_theme/header.tpl and if it doesn't exist, it will load /template/default/header.tpl. <h5>Module template file</h5> When you want to load a module template file, you must indicate the name of you module and then the name of the file like this: module/file.tpl which will load the /module/templates/file.tpl. If the user themes redefines the file.tpl file for the module module, the file templates/your_theme/modules/module/file.tpl will be loaded. <h5>Menu template file</h5> To load a file of a menu, use this kind of path: menus/my_menu/file.tpl which will load the /menus/my_menu/templates/file.tpl file. <h5>Framework template file</h5> To load a framework file, use a path like this: framework/package/file.tpl which will load /templates/your_theme/framework/package/file.tpl if the theme overrides it, otherwise /templates/default/framework/package/file.tpl will be used.

class StringTemplate extends AbstractTemplate io/template/StringTemplate.class.php

This class enables you to handle a template whose input is not a file but directly a string. To be always as efficient as possible, it uses cache if it evaluates that it could be faster. But when string templates are cached, they are saved on the filesystem and use some disk space. It's the reason why there is an option enabling to forbid it to cache a template if you think that it's not required to have a big efficiency. It will be the case for instance when you know that a string template will be used only once a month.

Package: IO · Since: PHPBoost 3.0 - 2010 02 06 · Version: PHPBoost 6.1 - last update: 2016 10 28 · Authors: Benoit SAUTEL <ben.popeye, Julien BRISWALTER <j1.seth, Arnaud GENET <elenwii
Constants
DONT_USE_CACHE false
USE_CACHE_IF_FASTER true
► Methods (2)
public __construct($content, $use_cache = self::USE_CACHE_IF_FASTER)
This class enables you to handle a template whose input is not a file but directly a string. To be always as efficient as possible, it uses cache if it evaluates that it could be faster. But when string templates are cached, they are saved on the filesystem and use some disk space. It's the reason why there is an option enabling to forbid it to cache a template if you think that it's not required to have a big efficiency. It will be the case for instance when you know that a string template will be used only once a month.
private has_to_cache($content, $use_cache)
class Template extends View io/template/Template.class.php

This class represents a PHPBoost template. Templates are used to generate text which have a common structure. You just have to write your text with tags at the place you want to insert values and assign values in the objet, when you will display them, the tags will be replaced by the corresponding value. PHPBoost's template engine is home made and has its own syntax which is the described below: <h4>Variable assignation</h4> <h5>Simple variables</h5> A simple variable is accessible with the {NAME} syntax where NAME is its template name. If the variable is not assigned, nothing will be displayed (no error message). Simple variables are assigned by the assign_vars() method. <h4>Lang variables</h4> To speed the development up, you can avoid setting all the localized variables to use. You just have to associate one or more lang map (lang_identifier => localized lang) with the {

Package: IO · Since: PHPBoost 3.0 - 2010 02 03 · Version: PHPBoost 6.1 - last update: 2014 12 22 · Authors: Benoit SAUTEL <ben.popeye

If the variable is not assigned, nothing will be displayed (no error message). Simple variables are assigned by the assign_vars() method. <h4>Lang variables</h4> To speed the development up, you can avoid setting all the localized variables to use. You just have to associate one or more lang map (lang_identifier => localized lang) with the {
public add_subtemplate($identifier, Template $template)
This class represents a PHPBoost template. Templates are used to generate text which have a common structure. You just have to write your text with tags at the place you want to insert values and assign values in the objet, when you will display them, the tags will be replaced by the corresponding value. PHPBoost's template engine is home made and has its own syntax which is the described below: <h4>Variable assignation</h4> <h5>Simple variables</h5> A simple variable is accessible with the {NAME} syntax where NAME is its template name. If the variable is not assigned, nothing will be displayed (no error message). Simple variables are assigned by the assign_vars() method. <h4>Lang variables</h4> To speed the development up, you can avoid setting all the localized variables to use. You just have to associate one or more lang map (lang_identifier => localized lang) with the {
public set_data(TemplateData $data)
This class represents a PHPBoost template. Templates are used to generate text which have a common structure. You just have to write your text with tags at the place you want to insert values and assign values in the objet, when you will display them, the tags will be replaced by the corresponding value. PHPBoost's template engine is home made and has its own syntax which is the described below: <h4>Variable assignation</h4> <h5>Simple variables</h5> A simple variable is accessible with the {NAME} syntax where NAME is its template name. If the variable is not assigned, nothing will be displayed (no error message). Simple variables are assigned by the assign_vars() method. <h4>Lang variables</h4> To speed the development up, you can avoid setting all the localized variables to use. You just have to associate one or more lang map (lang_identifier => localized lang) with the {
public get_data()

class TemplateData io/template/data/TemplateData.class.php

This interface describes data which are assigned to a template. There are two types of data: <ul> <li>Variables: there are string that are directly displayed</li> <li>Templates: you can embed a template in another one. When a subtemplate is displayed, it's parsed using its own data and the result is inserted where the subtemplate is embedded.</li> </ul> A template contains global variables which are available in the whole template and local variable which are only defined into a loop. To define a loop, you just have to use assign_block_vars with the same loop identifier for each loop iteration. Loops can be nested and embed both variables and subtemplates.

Package: IO · Since: PHPBoost 3.0 - 2010 02 19 · Version: PHPBoost 6.1 - last update: 2021 04 30 · Authors: Benoit SAUTEL <ben.popeye, Julien BRISWALTER <j1.seth

To define a loop, you just have to use assign_block_vars with the same loop identifier for each loop iteration. Loops can be nested and embed both variables and subtemplates.

class CachedStringTemplateLoader implements TemplateLoader io/template/loader/CachedStringTemplateLoader.class.php

This loader is to use when you load a template whose source is directly a PHP string and not a file. It supports caching and saves cache files in the /cache/tpl directory, using a md5 hash to distinguish eache string input.

Package: IO · Since: PHPBoost 3.0 - 2010 02 06 · Version: PHPBoost 6.1 - last update: 2014 12 22 · Authors: Benoit SAUTEL <ben.popeye

class FileTemplateLoader implements TemplateLoader io/template/loader/FileTemplateLoader.class.php

This loader is the most used. It takes a file identifier as input. This identifier corresponds to a file that can be different from the user's theme. In fact, when it loads a template, its looks for it at several places. For a module template, it can be the default one which is in the /module/templates directory, but if the file is specialized by the theme, it loads it from the theme directory. All that is explained in the {

class StringTemplateLoader implements TemplateLoader io/template/loader/StringTemplateLoader.class.php

This loader is a very simple one. Its input is a string containing the template source. It doesn't supports caching so it always parses the input source.

class TemplateLoader io/template/loader/TemplateLoader.class.php

This class is responsible to load a template and transform it in the PHPBoost parsed template syntax. Normally a loader is able to load a template and provide its parsed form. The parsing is done by a TemplateParser it embeds and is a quite heavy operation. To be more efficient, we've introduced the parsed files caching which enables us to parse each file only once and then reuse the cache. Even if the {

Package: IO · Since: PHPBoost 3.0 - 2009 06 18 · Version: PHPBoost 6.1 - last update: 2014 12 22 · Authors: Loic ROUCHON <horn, Benoit SAUTEL <ben.popeye

class TemplateParser io/template/parser/TemplateParser.class.php

class TemplateSyntaxParser implements TemplateParser io/template/parser/syntax/TemplateSyntaxParser.class.php

template = (variableExpression | expression | condition | loop | include | text)* variableExpression = "{", variable, "}" expression = "${", expressionContent, "}" condition = "# IF ", "NOT "?, expression, "#", template, ("# ELSEIF ", "NOT "?, expression, "#", template)*, ("# ELSE #", template)?, "# ENDIF #" loop = "# START ", expression, " #", template, "# END (?:name)? #" include = "# INCLUDE ", name, " #" text = .+ expressionContent = array | function | variable | constant function = "\(\w+::\)?\w+\(", parameters, "\)" parameters = expressionContent | (expressionContent, (",", expressionContent)+) variable = simpleVar | loopVar constant = "'.+'" | [0-9]+ | array array = "[", arrayContent, "]" arrayContent = arrayElement | (arrayElement, (",", arrayElement)+) arrayElement = expressionContent | ("'\w+'\s*=>\s*", expressionContent) simpleVar = "\w+" loopVar = "(\w+\.)+\w+"

Package: IO · Since: PHPBoost 3.0 - 2010 06 17 · Version: PHPBoost 6.1 - last update: 2026 03 04 · Authors: Benoit SAUTEL <ben.popeye, Loic ROUCHON <horn, Sebastien LARTIGUE <babsolune
► Methods (2)

class DefaultTemplateRenderer implements TemplateRenderer io/template/renderer/DefaultTemplateRenderer.class.php

This template renderer is able to deal with both loader which cache and don't cache. When the loader supports caching, it includes the cached file to use APC's optimization, but if it doesn't support it, it simply evals the template code.

Package: IO · Since: PHPBoost 3.0 - 2010 02 06 · Version: PHPBoost 6.1 - last update: 2016 10 28 · Authors: Benoit SAUTEL <ben.popeye, Julien BRISWALTER <j1.seth, Arnaud GENET <elenwii
► Methods (6)
public __construct()
public render(TemplateData $data, TemplateLoader $loader)
This template renderer is able to deal with both loader which cache and don't cache. When the loader supports caching, it includes the cached file to use APC's optimization, but if it doesn't support it, it simply evals the template code.
public add_lang(array $lang)
This template renderer is able to deal with both loader which cache and don't cache. When the loader supports caching, it includes the cached file to use APC's optimization, but if it doesn't support it, it simply evals the template code.
private parse(TemplateLoader $loader, TemplateData $data)
private execute(TemplateLoader $loader, TemplateData $data)
private get_code_to_eval(TemplateLoader $loader)
class TemplateRenderer io/template/renderer/TemplateRenderer.class.php

Represents a template renderer as its names shows. Its able to get the result of the template interpration from a TemplateLoader which gives it the template source and a TemplateData which contains the data to assign in the template.

Package: IO · Since: PHPBoost 3.0 - 2010 02 06 · Version: PHPBoost 6.1 - last update: 2014 12 22 · Authors: Benoit SAUTEL <ben.popeye
► Methods (2)
public render(TemplateData $data, TemplateLoader $loader)

public add_lang(array $lang)

class UnreadContributionsCache implements CacheData phpboost/cache/UnreadContributionsCache.class.php

This cache is used to know if there are unread contributions. If there are, it's able to tell us how much there are for the administrator, and for the other types of users, it's only able to tell if there are unread contributions.

class LastUseDateConfig extends AbstractConfigData phpboost/config/LastUseDateConfig.class.php

This class contains the date of the last day where PHPBoost was used. It's useful to know when to launch daily tasks.

class BreadCrumb phpboost/environment/BreadCrumb.class.php

This class is used to represent the bread crumb displayed on each page of the website. It enables the user to locate himself in the whole site. A bread crumb can look like this: Home >> My module >> First level category >> Second level category >> Third level category >> .. >> My page >> Edition

Package: PHPBoost · Since: PHPBoost 1.6 - 2007 02 16 · Version: PHPBoost 6.1 - last update: 2020 01 23 · Authors: Benoit SAUTEL <ben.popeye, Julien BRISWALTER <j1.seth, Arnaud GENET <elenwii

class AdministratorAlert extends Event phpboost/event/AdministratorAlert.class.php

This class represents an alert which must be sent to the administrator. It allows to the module developers to handle the administrator alerts. The administrator alerts can be in the administration panel and can be used when you want to signal an important event to the administrator(s).

Package: PHPBoost · Since: PHPBoost 2.0 - 2008 08 27 · Version: PHPBoost 6.1 - last update: 2021 12 16 · Authors: Benoit SAUTEL <ben.popeye, Julien BRISWALTER <j1.seth, Arnaud GENET <elenwii, Sebastien LARTIGUE <babsolune

ADMIN_ALERT_HIGH_PRIORITY 4
ADMIN_ALERT_VERY_HIGH_PRIORITY 5

ADMIN_ALERT_STATUS_PROCESSED Event::EVENT_STATUS_PROCESSED

class Contribution extends Event phpboost/event/Contribution.class.php

class Event phpboost/event/Event.class.php

It's the common part between two types of event existing now in PHPBoost: <ul> <li>User contribution managed into the contribution panel</li> <li>Administrator alert, triggered for example when a new update is available or when a new member account is to approbate</li> </ul>

Package: PHPBoost · Since: PHPBoost 2.0 - 2008 07 21 · Version: PHPBoost 6.1 - last update: 2021 11 18 · Authors: Benoit SAUTEL <ben.popeye, Arnaud GENET <elenwii, Sebastien LARTIGUE <babsolune

EVENT_STATUS_BEING_PROCESSED 1
EVENT_STATUS_PROCESSED 2

class ExtensionPointProvider phpboost/extension-provider/ExtensionPointProvider.class.php

This Class allow you to call methods on a ExtensionPointProvider extended class that you're not sure of the method's availality. It also provides a set of generic methods that you could use to integrate your module with others, or allow your module to share services.

class ActionAuthorization phpboost/member/authorization/ActionAuthorization.class.php

This class represents the authorizations for an action. It's associated to a label, a description, the bit in which flags are saved, and obviously the authorization array which is encapsulated in the RolesAuthorizations class. The bit which is used to store the authorization is 2^n where n is the number of the place you want to use. It's recommanded to begin with 1 (2^0 = 1) then 2 (2^1 = 2) then 4 (2^2 = 4) etc...

Package: PHPBoost · Since: PHPBoost 3.0 - 2010 03 01 · Version: PHPBoost 6.1 - last update: 2025 11 27 · Authors: Benoit SAUTEL <ben.popeye, Julien BRISWALTER <j1.seth, Sebastien LARTIGUE <babsolune

class AuthorizationsSettings phpboost/member/authorization/AuthorizationsSettings.class.php

This class manages authorizations settings which deals with all the actions for which you want to restrict access. You can choose who can access to between the different roles existing in PHPBoost: <ul> <li>ranks (guest, member, moderator, administrator)</li> <li>groups (members can belong to one or more groups)</li> <li>members (you can tell that only a particular user can access)</li> </ul> This class contains a list of {

class MemberDisabledActionAuthorization extends ActionAuthorization phpboost/member/authorization/MemberDisabledActionAuthorization.class.php

This class represents the authorizations for an action. It's associated to a label, a description, the bit in which flags are saved, and obviously the authorization array which is encapsulated in the RolesAuthorizations class. The bit which is used to store the authorization is 2^n where n is the number of the place you want to use. It's recommanded to begin with 1 (2^0 = 1) then 2 (2^1 = 2) then 4 (2^2 = 4) etc... In this class the select of Visitor and Member level is not possible.

Package: PHPBoost · Since: PHPBoost 5.1 - 2018 11 04 · Version: PHPBoost 6.1 - last update: 2025 11 27 · Authors: Julien BRISWALTER <j1.seth, Sebastien LARTIGUE <babsolune
► Methods (1)

This class represents the authorizations for an action. It's associated to a label, a description, the bit in which flags are saved, and obviously the authorization array which is encapsulated in the RolesAuthorizations class. The bit which is used to store the authorization is 2^n where n is the number of the place you want to use. It's recommanded to begin with 1 (2^0 = 1) then 2 (2^1 = 2) then 4 (2^2 = 4) etc... In this class the select of Visitor and Member level is not possible.

class ExtendedFieldsDatabaseService phpboost/member/extended-fields/ExtendedFieldsDatabaseService.class.php

This class is responsible of all database accesses implied by the extended fields management. Indeed, for instance when a field is created, the data base structure must be updated throw an ALTER request.

class Menu phpboost/menu/Menu.class.php

This class represents a menu element and is used to build any kind of menu

Package: PHPBoost · Since: PHPBoost 2.0 - 2008 11 15 · Version: PHPBoost 6.1 - last update: 2021 10 11 · Authors: Loic ROUCHON <horn, Julien BRISWALTER <j1.seth, Sebastien LARTIGUE <babsolune

MENU_AUTH_BIT 1
MENU_ENABLE_OR_NOT 42
MENU_ENABLED true
MENU_NOT_ENABLED false
BLOCK_POSITION__NOT_ENABLED 0
BLOCK_POSITION__HEADER 1
BLOCK_POSITION__SUB_HEADER 2
BLOCK_POSITION__TOP_CENTRAL 3
BLOCK_POSITION__BOTTOM_CENTRAL 4
BLOCK_POSITION__TOP_FOOTER 5
BLOCK_POSITION__FOOTER 6
BLOCK_POSITION__LEFT 7
BLOCK_POSITION__RIGHT 8
BLOCK_POSITION__TOP_HEADER 9
BLOCK_POSITION__ALL 10
PUSHMENU_LEFT 'left'
PUSHMENU_RIGHT 'right'
PUSHMENU_TOP 'top'
PUSHMENU_BOTTOM 'bottom'
PUSHMENU_OVERLAP 'overlap'
PUSHMENU_EXPAND 'expand'
PUSHMENU_NONE 'false'

class MenuService phpboost/menu/MenuService.class.php

This service manage kernel menus by adding the persistance to menus objects. It also provides all moving and disabling methods to change the website appearance.

class AuthenticationMethod phpboost/user/authentication/AuthenticationMethod.class.php

The AuthenticationMethod interface could be implemented in different ways to enable specifics authentication mecanisms. PHPBoost comes with a PHPBoostAuthenticationMethod which will be performed on the internal member list. But it is possible to implement external authentication mecanism by providing others implementations of this class to support LDAP authentication, OpenID, Facebook connect and more...

Package: PHPBoost · Since: PHPBoost 3.0 - 2010 11 28 · Version: PHPBoost 6.1 - last update: 2021 12 16 · Authors: Loic ROUCHON <horn, Julien BRISWALTER <j1.seth, Sebastien LARTIGUE <babsolune
► Methods (7)

class AuthenticationService phpboost/user/authentication/AuthenticationService.class.php

This class manages the authentication mecanism. Several authentication methods could be used. If the authentication by the selected method successful, the user session is started.

class Date util/Date.class.php

This class allows you to handle easily some dates. A date is a day and an hour (year, month, day, hour, minutes, seconds). It supports the most common formats and manages timezones. Here are the definitions of the 3 existing timezones: <ul> <li>System timezone: it's the timezone of the server, configured by the hoster. For instance, if your server is in France, it should be GMT+1.</li> <li>Site timezone: it's the timezone of the central place of the site. For example, if your site deals with the italian soccer championship, it will be GMT+1.</li> <li>User timezone : each registered user can specify its timezone. It's particulary useful for people who visit some sites from a foreign country.</li> </ul>

Package: Util · Since: PHPBoost 2.0 - 2008 06 01 · Version: PHPBoost 6.1 - last update: 2026 01 25 · Authors: Benoit SAUTEL <ben.popeye, Julien BRISWALTER <j1.seth, Arnaud GENET <elenwii, mipel <mipel, Sebastien LARTIGUE <babsolune
Constants
DATE_NOW 'now'
FORMAT_TIMESTAMP 0
FORMAT_DAY_MONTH 1
FORMAT_DAY_MONTH_YEAR 2
FORMAT_DAY_MONTH_YEAR_HOUR_MINUTE 3
FORMAT_RFC2822 4
FORMAT_ISO8601 5
FORMAT_DAY_MONTH_YEAR_LONG 6
FORMAT_DAY_MONTH_YEAR_TEXT 7
FORMAT_DAY_MONTH_YEAR_HOUR_MINUTE_TEXT 8
FORMAT_RELATIVE 9
FORMAT_ISO_DAY_MONTH_YEAR 10
FORMAT_AGO 11
FORMAT_SINCE 12
FORMAT_DELAY 13
FORMAT_HOUR_MINUTE 14
FORMAT_DAY_MONTH_TEXT 15

public set_week_number(int $week_number): void
public get_day(int $timezone = Timezone::USER_TIMEZONE): string
This class allows you to handle easily some dates. A date is a day and an hour (year, month, day, hour, minutes, seconds). It supports the most common formats and manages timezones. Here are the definitions of the 3 existing timezones: <ul> <li>System timezone: it's the timezone of the server, configured by the hoster. For instance, if your server is in France, it should be GMT+1.</li> <li>Site timezone: it's the timezone of the central place of the site. For example, if your site deals with the italian soccer championship, it will be GMT+1.</li> <li>User timezone : each registered user can specify its timezone. It's particulary useful for people who visit some sites from a foreign country.</li> </ul>
public get_day_two_digits(int $timezone = Timezone::USER_TIMEZONE): string
public set_day(int $day, int $referential_timezone = Timezone::USER_TIMEZONE): void
public get_day_text(string $characters_number = '', int $timezone = Timezone::USER_TIMEZONE): string

It's particulary useful for people who visit some sites from a foreign country.</li> </ul>
public get_day_of_week(int $timezone = Timezone::USER_TIMEZONE): int

class MiniCalendar util/MiniCalendar.class.php

This class enables you to retrieve easily a date entered by a user. If the user isn't in the same timezone as the server, the hour will be automatically recomputed.

Package: Util · Since: PHPBoost 2.0 - 2008 06 03 · Version: PHPBoost 6.1 - last update: 2025 11 27 · Authors: Benoit SAUTEL <ben.popeye, Julien BRISWALTER <j1.seth, Arnaud GENET <elenwii, Sebastien LARTIGUE <babsolune

class PasswordHasher util/PasswordHasher.class.php

Password hashing utility class that manages password hashing and verification. Supports progressive migration from MD5/SHA256 to bcrypt.

BCRYPT_COST 12

class Url util/Url.class.php
Constants
FORBID_JS_REGEX '(?!javascript:)'
PROTOCOL_REGEX '[a-z0-9-_]+(?::[a-z0-9-_]+)*://'
USER_REGEX '[a-z0-9-_]+(?::[a-z0-9-_]+)?@'
DOMAIN_REGEX '(?:[a-z0-9-_~]+\.)*[a-z0-9-_~]+(?::[0-9]{1,5})?/'
FOLDERS_REGEX '/*(?:[A-Za-z0-9~_\.+@,-]+/+)*'
FILE_REGEX '[A-Za-z0-9-+_,~:/\.\%!=]+'
ARGS_REGEX '(/([\w/_\.#-]*(\?)?(\S+)?[^\.\s])?)?'
STATUS_OK 200
► Methods (25)
public __construct(string $url = '.', ?string $path_to_root = null, ?string $server_url = null)
This class offers a simple way to transform an absolute or relative link to a relative one to the website root. It can also deals with absolute url and will convert only those from this site into relatives ones. Usage : <ul> <li>In content, get the url with the absolute() method. It will allow content include at multiple level</li> <li>In forms, get the url with the relative() method. It's a faster way to display url</li> </ul>
public is_relative(): bool
This class offers a simple way to transform an absolute or relative link to a relative one to the website root. It can also deals with absolute url and will convert only those from this site into relatives ones. Usage : <ul> <li>In content, get the url with the absolute() method. It will allow content include at multiple level</li> <li>In forms, get the url with the relative() method. It's a faster way to display url</li> </ul>

This class offers a simple way to transform an absolute or relative link to a relative one to the website root. It can also deals with absolute url and will convert only those from this site into relatives ones. Usage : <ul> <li>In content, get the url with the absolute() method. It will allow content include at multiple level</li> <li>In forms, get the url with the relative() method. It's a faster way to display url</li> </ul>
static html_convert_root_relative2relative(string $html_text, string $path_to_root = PATH_TO_ROOT, string $server_url = SERVER_URL): string

It can also deals with absolute url and will convert only those from this site into relatives ones. Usage : <ul> <li>In content, get the url with the absolute() method. It will allow content include at multiple level</li> <li>In forms, get the url with the relative() method. It's a faster way to display url</li> </ul>
static get_relative(string $url, ?string $path_to_root = null, ?string $server_url = null): string
